reasoning: The ICE BofA Single-B effective yield spiked heavily in early April 2025, peaking at 8.71% on April 7 due to Trump's tariff announcements causing a massive widening in high-yield spreads. On April 9, a 90-day tariff pause was announced, leading to an immediate drop in yields to 8.55%, closing Friday April 11 at 8.57%. As panic subsides during the 90-day pause, high-yield spreads are expected to mean-revert and compress toward historical averages, pulling the effective yield down over the coming weeks. Therefore, the short-term probability (April 20, May 13) of the yield remaining above 8.57% is relatively low. The 90-day pause expires around July 8, introducing significant upside risk of a renewed tariff panic right before the July 12 resolution date, warranting a higher probability. By October 10, increasing macroeconomic volatility and possible Fed rate cuts make the outcome more uncertain, bringing the probability closer to 0.5 in accordance with instructions.
